articles.title: Under the Hood of MDN's New Frontend
articles.description: The hugely useful MDN has rebuilt its frontend stack from the ground up, ditching React for web components and a homegrown server component system. A great read on building a modern, content-heavy site without shipping unnecessary JavaScript on every page.
articles.author: Leo McArdle (MDN)

articles.title: 🕹️ Phaser 4.0: The 2D WebGL and Canvas-Based Game Framework
articles.description: The widely used game framework celebrates its 13th birthday with a major release focused on perf/efficiency improvements, and includes skills files so AI agents can build Phaser 4.0 apps well. There are lots of demos, including these games, and existing users get a v3 to v4 migration guide.
articles.author: Phaser Studio Inc.

articles.title: Installing Every Firefox Extension
articles.description: One person’s entertaining and heroic tale of wielding JavaScript to explore the Firefox extension ecosystem. And what oddities there are within! I enjoyed this a lot, it’s like Alice in Wonderland for developers. More spelunking like this please.
articles.author: Jack Cab

articles.title: Uses for Nested Promises
articles.description: James revisits 2013's Promises/A+ monads debate and has changed his mind, thanks to a real concurrency problem he ran into. Demanding but rewarding.
articles.author: James Coglan

articles.title: You Can't Cancel a Promise (Except Sometimes You Can)
articles.description: You can’t cancel a promise, but you can halt an async function by making it await a promise that never resolves. The function silently stops, and GC cleans up after it.
articles.author: Aaron Harper (Inngest)

articles.title: Parse, Don't Validate (In a Language That Doesn't Want You To)
articles.description: Tired of writing the same defensive if check in multiple files because you can’t trust that validation already happened? Branded types and discriminated unions can let TypeScript carry that proof for you.
articles.author: Christian Ekrem

articles.title: 🌐 The Intl API: The Best Browser API You’re Not Using
articles.description: A neat code-heavy primer to what you can do with Intl.
articles.author: Kilian Valkhof

articles.title: Boneyard: Auto-Generated Skeleton Screens for Your UI
articles.description: Snapshots your real UI and captures a flat list of skeleton ‘bones’ which are positioned, sized rectangles that mirror the page exactly. Supports React, Preact, React Native, Vue, Svelte, and Angular.
articles.author: 0xGF

articles.title: 📈 Micro-ML: A Toolkit of Forecasting and Clustering Algorithms
articles.description: A ~56KB WASM-powered library with algorithms for regression and smoothing. Cluster points, classify data, or predict the next value in a series without dragging in TensorFlow.js.
articles.author: Adam Perliński

articles.title: Ink 7.0: Use React to Build TUIs and Command Line Apps
articles.description: Powering many popular terminal apps, v7.0 now leans on React 19.2, uses useEffectEvent internally for added efficiency, and brings new hooks and settings.
articles.author: Vadim Demedes

articles.title: 🔊 web-audio-api: Use the Web Audio API from Node and Bun
articles.description: Full Web Audio API support to either play audio on your machine/server or render it to file (and, yes, Tone.js works too). There are many examples to enjoy.
articles.author: Sébastien Piquemal

articles.title: Syncpack: Consistent Dependency Versions in Large JS Monorepos
articles.description: A CLI tool (used by Electron, Cloudflare, Vercel and others) that finds version mismatches across your entire monorepo, fixes them, and can enforce version policies in CI to avoid future drift.
articles.author: Jamie Mason

articles.title: Windows 95 as an Electron App
articles.description: A full Windows 95 experience as an app on macOS, Linux, and Windows, built upon the v86 JavaScript + WASM emulator. v5.0 is a big release as you can mount a folder from your machine into it as a Z: drive, mount ISOs as CD-ROMs, there’s a shared clipboard, and Internet access has been improved. I’m so trying to get Microsoft Encarta’s Mindmaze running on this…
articles.author: Felix Rieseberg

articles.description: Google will penalize sites that 'hijack' the back button in its search results from June. "Ensure you are not doing anything to interfere with a user's ability to navigate their browser history," says Chris Nelson.
TanStack Start now has (experimental) React Server Components support.
🇫🇷 dotJS returns to Paris, France this September 18 – its CFP is open for two more weeks if you'd like to speak.
🇷🇴 The JSHeroes conference is back this May 14-15 in Romania.
Bun v1.3.12 – The JS runtime now ships with native, headless browser automation built in, and Bun.cron provides an in-process task scheduler.
⚠️ React 19.2.5, 19.1.6 and 19.0.5 have been released to deploy a fix for a React Server Components vulnerability.
React Native 0.85 – New animation backend and devtools improvements.
pnpm v11.0 RC 0, React Three Fiber 9.6, Electron 41.2, DOMPurify 3.4

articles.title: Google's New Spam Policy for "Back Button Hijacking"
articles.description: Starting in mid June, Google Search will begin penalizing sites that ‘hijack’ the back button. “Ensure you are not doing anything to interfere with a user’s ability to navigate their browser history”.
articles.author: Chris Nelson (Google)

articles.title: Under the Hood of MDN's New Frontend
articles.description: MDN is one of the most useful resources we turn to as frontend devs and it had a major facelift last year. This post digs into the architecture, why it opted to rebuild, and why React was removed in favor of a web components-based approach.
articles.author: Leo McArdle (MDN)

articles.title: 🤖 Agentic Engine Optimization (AEO)
articles.description: You’re familiar with SEO, but the idea of optimizing sites to be discovered and used by agents and other AI-adjacent tools is rapidly catching on too. Google’s Addy Osmani shares some pointers for the basic techniques involved.
articles.author: Addy Osmani

articles.title: SVG Filters Guide: Getting Started with the Basics
articles.description: Explains how to set up SVG filters, along with filter regions, color interpolation, and more, all whilst getting consistent cross-browser results.
articles.author: Ana Tudor

articles.title: Squash and Stretch
articles.description: Josh shares how he’s been taking cues from a set of classic Disney animation principles to make motion effects feel more satisfying. He details how he’s applied the techniques on the web, with the end result gaining an extra level of polish.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: 🤖 Why AI Sucks at Front End
articles.description: A rant on the pros and numerous cons of AI design — stuff that’s trained on “ancient solutions, unoriginal UI patterns, and watered down junk”.
articles.author: Adam Argyle

articles.title: Unicode Variation Selectors
articles.description: Did you know about the font-variant-emoji property? It provides a way to control whether a Unicode glyph or its emoji counterpart is displayed.
articles.author: Matthias Zöchling

articles.title: Name-Only @container Queries: A Solution to the Naming Wars
articles.description: An introduction to name-only @container queries (in Safari only, for now). This addition makes naming much easier with a feature that improves scoping without adding specificity.
articles.author: Saron Yitbarek (WebKit)

articles.title: The Radio (Button) State Machine
articles.description: Typically managing state with JavaScript is the right approach — but how would you go about doing it in CSS? Amit explores keeping the logic in CSS, via :has(), transforms, variables, etc. A curious bit of experimentation if nothing else.
articles.author: Amit Sheen

articles.title: Container Query Typography Systems
articles.description: Begone breakpoint-based type scales! Here’s how to use container queries and the :has selector to create container-based scales.
articles.author: Matt Waler

articles.title: The Intl API: The Best Browser API You’re Not Using
articles.description: Or maybe you are. Either way, here's a neat primer to what you can do with Intl.
articles.author: Kilian Valkhof

articles.title: Charcuterie: Visually Explore Unicode Characters
articles.description: A browser-based smorgasbord of Unicode characters and emojis, all sorted by their ‘visual similarities’. Click around to get the idea or you can type (or draw!) your own character in the fields at the top to find the right character for your use case.
articles.author: David Aerne

articles.title: og-check: Test OpenGraph Locally Before You Go Public
articles.description: If you’re building locally, tools like Facebook’s Sharing Debugger won’t work, but this new command line tool lets you test your OpenGraph tags locally and see what the public will see before you deploy it.
articles.author: Simon Hartcher

articles.title: 🕹️ Phaser 4.0: The 2D WebGL/Canvas-Based Game Framework
articles.description: The widely used game framework celebrates thirteen years with a major release focused on perf/efficiency improvements, as well as skills files so AI agents can build modern Phaser apps. There are hundreds of demos to show it off.
articles.author: Phaser Studio Inc.

articles.title: oklchanger: A VS Code Extension to Convert Colors to OKLCH
articles.description: Has support for converting color keywords, hex, RGB(A), HSL(A), Lab, and LCH, done via the command palette.
articles.author: Silvestar Bistrović

articles.title: SLITSCANNER: Image and Text Distortion Art Creation Tool
articles.description: If you’re familiar with slit-scan photography, this will make sense. Upload an image, set some scan settings, then move the image around while the ‘scanning’ takes place, to get the effect. A neat aesthetic for background images, perhaps.
articles.author: Matt Soria

articles.title: delphitools: 40+ Simple Online Tools
articles.description: A charming collection of “small, low stakes and low effort tools”. Like a watermarker, palette generator, background remover, image converter, SVG optimizer, line-height calculator, Tailwind CSS shade generator, and many more. All with no logins or tracking.
articles.author: Ruby morgan voigt
